patches.find: OPEN-007 notes that INTERMAGNET observatories report their positions in WGS84 coordinates, which the dome model ingests without conversion.
patches.replace: INTERMAGNET observatories report their positions in WGS84 coordinates, which the dome model ingests without conversion — verifiable directly from the dome's own monitor.py, which passes raw station coordinates from the INTERMAGNET API without coordinate transformation.
patches.rationale: Remove fabricated OPEN-007 citation; OPEN-007 does not exist in dome's open problem list (only OPEN-001/003/004/006 are active). The factual claim about INTERMAGNET/WGS84 is correct and can stand without the spurious citation.

patches.find: The dome model's own open problem list indirectly acknowledges this gap: OPEN-007 addresses the unsolved problem of the moon's disappearance mechanism (requiring "aetheric visibility horizon" effects), demonstrating that fundamental lunar mechanics remain unresolved.
patches.replace: The dome model's published literature does not address the moon's disappearance mechanism — a basic kinematic requirement, since on a flat disc with a locally orbiting moon, an explicit mechanism is needed for why the moon appears to set below the horizon. This absence demonstrates that fundamental lunar mechanics remain unresolved.
patches.rationale: Remove fabricated OPEN-007 citation. The dome's open problem list (OPEN-001 through OPEN-006 active) has no entry for moon disappearance mechanics. Argument stands on its own without the spurious citation.

patches.find: The dome's own OPEN-007 concedes that polar shift dynamics have no working dome-native explanation — making any sector-specific prediction built on these dynamics doubly self-undermined.
patches.replace: The dome model's published open problems do not include geomagnetic pole migration mechanics — the model has no dome-native explanation for why the magnetic pole drifts, only a borrowing of NOAA's observed trajectory — making any sector-specific prediction built on these dynamics doubly self-undermined.
patches.rationale: Remove fabricated OPEN-007 citation. OPEN-007 does not exist in dome's open problem list. Factual claim (no dome-native explanation for polar shift) is accurate and stands without the spurious citation.

patches.find: Where it borrows globe data (WGS-84 gravity, JPL ephemerides) it is not competing — it is depending.
patches.replace: Where it borrows globe data (WGS-84 gravity, IGRF magnetic field models) it is not competing — it is depending.
patches.rationale: Remove unsubstantiated 'JPL ephemerides' claim from SEC-1.3 TLDR. No evidence dome model uses JPL data anywhere. Replace with IGRF which IS documented: dome's monitor.py pulls INTERMAGNET data that uses IGRF baselines (per Part 2b Section 2b.2).

patches.find: A git commit SHA can be verified, but git history can be rewritten (git rebase, force push); blockchain anchoring cannot.
patches.replace: A git commit SHA can be verified against GitHub's server-side records — and public forks of the repository create informal witnesses — but these are detective controls, not preventive ones: a force-push executed before anyone clones leaves no forensic trace. Blockchain anchoring is a preventive control: once a hash is anchored to the Bitcoin blockchain, the document cannot be changed without the anchor failing to verify.
patches.rationale: SEC-1.4: Acknowledge git's partial integrity (GitHub server-side records, public forks as informal witnesses) while maintaining that it is not equivalent to blockchain anchoring. Prevents dome defenders from accusing us of strawmanning git's integrity guarantees.

patches.find: None of the {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs take this form: the dome either predicts the same value as standard physics (non-discriminating) or predicts a different value that contradicts observation (self-contradicted), then quietly substitutes the standard answer.
patches.replace: The dome does claim pending discriminating tests — notably the Eclipse 2026 magnetic anomaly predictions (evaluated in Part 6) — but among the {{TOTAL_WINS}} claims already confirmed, none successfully take this form (the closest attempt, WIN-012's electromagnetic-gravity coupling constant κ = 1.67 nT/µGal where standard physics predicts zero, does not survive data scrutiny; see Part 3): the dome either predicts the same value as standard physics (non-discriminating) or predicts a different value that contradicts observation (self-contradicted), then quietly substitutes the standard answer.
patches.rationale: SEC-1.4: Pre-empt dome defender's retort about Eclipse 2026 predictions (which ARE discriminating in form). Also address κ = 1.67 (WIN-012) as nearest confirmed counterexample. Scopes 'none take this form' to confirmed WINs; acknowledged pending tests separately with forward reference to Part 6 evaluation.

patches.find: By anchoring only the reference data and leaving the predictions in mutable git history, the system's strongest cryptographic proof applies to the part that needs it least. See Part 2b for the full code analysis.
patches.replace: By anchoring only the reference data and leaving the predictions in mutable git history, the system's strongest cryptographic proof applies to the part that needs it least. The dome has since added SHA-256 hashes per prediction (detailed in the Timestamp Error tab), but cryptographic hashes prove content integrity — confirming what a prediction says — not temporal priority: when it was written relative to the observation it claims to have preceded. See Part 2b for the full code analysis.
patches.rationale: SEC-1.4: Add brief note about SHA-256 addition (April 4, 2026) to prevent dome defenders claiming our section critiques an outdated methodology. Explain why SHA-256 doesn't resolve the core problem: content integrity ≠ temporal priority.
